course_position.previous_lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
course_position.next_lecture_title: Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)

summary_lines: Kilderne introducerer narrativ psykologi, hvor personlighed forstås gennem fortælling, tid og kulturel meningsdannelse.
Forelæsningen kontrasterer narrative perspektiver med træk- og mekanismeorienterede modeller.
Identitet fremstilles som noget, der løbende skabes og omskrives i sociale fortællepraksisser.
key_points: Narrativer organiserer hukommelse, motivforståelse og oplevet kontinuitet.
Livsfortællinger samskabes i kulturelle og relationelle sammenhænge.
Analyse af plot, stemme og positionering er central i narrativ metode.
Forandring kan beskrives som narrativ revision over tid.

sidecars.content:
## Shared problem
*   **Moving beyond static models of personality:** All texts grapple with how to conceptualize human identity as dynamic, contextual, and meaning-driven, challenging the sufficiency of traditional, decontextualized trait psychology (e.g., the Big Five) to capture the "whole person."
*   **The mechanism of meaning-making:** The authors collectively explore how individuals synthesize chaotic life events, cultural norms, and biological predispositions into coherent (or deliberately plural) frameworks, specifically through the psychological tool of *narrative*.

## Cross-reading distinctions and tensions
*   **Integrative vs. Plural/Decentered Self:** McAdams views the "integrative life story" as the pinnacle of healthy modern identity, functioning to unify the self. Conversely, Ragg

readings.title: Grundbog kapitel 09 - Narrative teorier
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L2 Narrativitet, kultur og mening/W11L2 Grundbog kapitel 9 - Narrative teorier.pdf

summary_lines: Kapitlet forklarer personlighed via narrativ identitet på tværs af fortid, nutid og forventet fremtid.
Fortællinger skaber sammenhæng, men synliggør også brud, konflikter og vendepunkter.
Kulturelle fortælleskabeloner både åbner og begrænser mulige selvforståelser.
key_points: Selvet opretholdes gennem kontinuerligt narrativt arbejde.
Narrativ analyse fokuserer på genre, plot og positionering.
Kollektive fortællinger former individuelle identitetsmuligheder.
Forandring kan forstås som omorganisering af livsfortællingen.

sidecars.relative_path: Readings/W11L2 Narrativitet, kultur og mening/W11L2 Grundbog kapitel 9 - Narrative teorier.analysis.md
sidecars.content:
## Core distinctions
*   **Information Processing vs. Meaning-Making:** Bruner distinguishes traditional cognitive psychology (which treats the mind as a passive computer processing information) from cultural-narrative psychology (which treats humans as active creators of meaning using culturally available narrative tools).
*

readings.title: Bruner (1999)
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L2 Narrativitet, kultur og mening/W11L2 Bruner (1999).pdf

summary_lines: Bruner argues that human meaning is fundamentally narrative and culturally organized.
He links everyday folk psychology to how people interpret action, intention, and disruption.
Narrative framing is presented as central for making persons intelligible in social life.
key_points: Meaning-making depends on culturally shared narrative forms.
Folk psychology provides interpretive background for agency and motive.
Narratives normalize and problematize conduct through plot structures.
Identity emerges through storied interpretation of experience.

readings.title: McAdams & Pals (2006)
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L2 Narrativitet, kultur og mening/W11L2 McAdams & Pals (2006).pdf

summary_lines: McAdams and Pals propose an integrative model that combines dispositional traits, characteristic adaptations, and life narratives.
They argue that personality science advances by linking universal tendencies to contextualized individual lives.
The article offers a synthesis across biological, developmental, and cultural levels of analysis.
key_points: Traits, adaptations, and narratives represent distinct but connected levels.
Personality explanation requires both structure and process.
Culture and development shape how dispositional potentials are expressed.
Integration across levels is a core theoretical task.

readings.title: Raggatt (2002)
readings.subject_relative_path: Readings/W11L2 Narrativitet, kultur og mening/W11L2 Raggatt (2002).pdf

summary_lines: Raggatt develops a dialogical approach in which personality is organized through multiple internal and social voices.
The perspective treats identity as polyphonic, contested, and situated in communicative relations.
Personality is therefore understood as dynamic positioning rather than singular essence.
key_points: Selfhood contains multiple positionings that may conflict or cooperate.
Dialogue is both intrapsychic and interpersonal.
Identity stability is achieved through ongoing coordination work.
Methodologically, analysis tracks voice, position, and relation.
